perturbation.original_text: 12. This Contract shall be governed by Buyer country laws and its appropriate courts of Paris. It is expressly agreed that if any dispute should arise, the competent jurisdiction of Paris in France shall be the sole competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Any Proprietary Information and copies thereof disclosed by either Party to the other shall remain the property of the dis closing Party and shall be returned by the receiving Party immediately upon request. 12. This Contract shall be governed by Buyer country laws and its appropriate courts of Paris. It is expressly agreed that if any dispute should arise, the competent jurisdiction of Paris in France shall be the sole competent jurisdiction. The above provisions shall not apply to the Proprietary Information disclosed by the Supplier to the Buyer and which fall und er clause 12.2 of the General Purchase Conditions.
perturbation.explanation: The jurisdiction clause has been moved within the section describing the return of proprietary information, embedding it within a discussion of property rights rather than standing as an independent clause. This obscures its importance and makes it less visible.
perturbation.contradicted_law: French Code of Civil Procedure, Article 48
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 48 of the French Code of Civil Procedure requires that clauses conferring jurisdiction must be clearly and explicitly stated in a manner that leaves no ambiguity. By burying the jurisdiction clause within a paragraph discussing the return of proprietary information, the modification reduces its prominence and potentially introduces ambiguity, making it harder to argue that it was clearly and explicitly agreed upon.

perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that the jurisdiction clause has been moved and embedded within a discussion of property rights, obscuring its importance and making it less visible. The law explanation states that Article 48 of the French Code of Civil Procedure requires clauses conferring jurisdiction to be clearly and explicitly stated, and that burying the jurisdiction clause introduces ambiguity. The 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' definition indicates that a contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in a misleading section, making it difficult to interpret. While Scraped Law Snippet 2 is not helpful as it is just a wikipedia page, this change falls under this definition, so a contradiction exists.
